methods.method_name: Taxon Sampling and Sequence Data Preparation
methods.description: This method describes the process of selecting taxa, obtaining DNA sequences from specific chloroplast regions, and preparing the sequence data for phylogenetic analysis. It involves defining two distinct datasets (matrix A for higher-level analysis and matrix B for species-level analysis within Pseuduvaria), obtaining sequences from samples and public databases, assembling and aligning the sequences, and excluding ambiguous positions.

steps.description: Define two separate datasets: matrix A for higher-level analyses and matrix B for species-level analyses within Pseuduvaria. Both sets of analyses were based on chloroplast DNA sequences from five regions (psbA-trnH spacer, trnL-F, matK, rbcL and atpB-rbcL spacer).
steps.searchable_summary: Define phylogenetic datasets based on specific DNA regions
outputs: List of target DNA regions (psbA-trnH spacer, trnL-F, matK, rbcL, atpB-rbcL spacer)

steps.description: The sequences for the outgroups were downloaded from GenBank. The accession numbers for all sequences are presented in Additional File 1.
steps.searchable_summary: Download DNA sequences for outgroups from GenBank
outputs: DNA sequences (FASTA or similar format) for outgroups

steps.description: Matrix A comprised a total of 83 species. The ingroup included 22 of the total 56 Pseuduvaria species, representing all five main clades identified in our previous phylogenetic study [25], and 56 species from 46 different Annonaceae genera (inclusive of the basal grade, LBC and SBC). The outgroups included were Coelocaryon preussii (Myristicaceae), Eupomatia bennettii (Eupomatiaceae), Liriodendron chinense (Magnoliaceae), Magnolia kobus (Magnoliaceae), and Persea americana (Lauraceae).
steps.searchable_summary: Assemble Matrix A dataset with specified ingroup and outgroup taxa
inputs: DNA sequences for ingroup (Pseuduvaria and other Annonaceae)
DNA sequences for outgroups (Myristicaceae, Eupomatiaceae, Magnoliaceae, Lauraceae)
Previous phylogenetic study results [25] (for Pseuduvaria clade representation)
outputs: Matrix A dataset (raw sequences for specified taxa)

steps.description: Matrix B consisted of 69 taxa, with 54 Pseuduvaria species. The selection of outgroups was based on the phylogeny generated by BEAST using matrix A (Figure 2A). The SBC genera selected as outgroups were Alphonsea, Haplostichanthus, Miliusa, Monocarpia, Neo-uvaria, Orophea, Polyalthia, and Sapranthus, representing 15 species.
steps.searchable_summary: Assemble Matrix B dataset with specified ingroup and outgroup taxa based on a preliminary phylogeny
inputs: DNA sequences for selected SBC genera (Alphonsea, Haplostichanthus, etc.)
Phylogeny generated by BEAST using matrix A (Figure 2A)
outputs: Matrix B dataset (raw sequences for specified taxa)

steps.description: For matrices A and B, sequences were edited and assembled in SeqManPro using DNAStar Lasergene 8 (DNAStar).
steps.searchable_summary: Edit and assemble raw DNA sequences using SeqManPro
inputs: Raw DNA sequences for taxa in Matrix A and Matrix B

steps.description: Aligned manually using BioEdit ver. 7.09 [34] and Se-Al ver. 2.0a11 [35].
steps.searchable_summary: Manually align DNA sequences using BioEdit and Se-Al

steps.description: Ambiguously aligned positions were excluded from the analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Exclude ambiguously aligned positions from the sequence alignment
outputs: Cleaned DNA sequence alignment (ambiguous positions removed)

steps.description: The combined five-region data sets in matrices A and B are composed of 4860 and 4361 characters, respectively (see Table 1 for data on individual gene regions).
steps.searchable_summary: Report final character counts for the combined datasets
inputs: Cleaned DNA sequence alignment for Matrix A
Cleaned DNA sequence alignment for Matrix B
